scope.summary: Implement per-panel snapshot/archive API (archive_panel_state, restore_panel_state) backed by tailor_garment_versions so individual pattern pieces can be snapshotted and restored independently of a full garment promotion
acceptance_criteria: archive_panel_state(panel_id, label: String) snapshots the current spec_json of a single panel into a tailor_panel_snapshots row with a human-readable label and timestamp
restore_panel_state(snapshot_id) replaces the current panel spec_json with the snapshot and emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded with the restore diff
Snapshots are linked to the parent garment version chain (tailor_garment_versions FK) so garment promotion preserves them
list_panel_snapshots(panel_id) returns snapshot ids, labels, and timestamps via the REST API
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=archive and operation=restore
PANEL_CLOSED re-validates after restore; a restored snapshot that was already valid does not re-block promotion
scope.risk_if_missed: MT-077 versions full garments (promoted snapshots) and MT-139 versions GarmentSpec schemas. Neither provides the per-panel non-destructive iteration workflow MD Pattern Archive enables: quickly trying a neckline shape, reverting to the previous one, and trying again without re-running a full garment promotion. Without this, every authoring experiment requires a full garment version commit, making iterative panel design expensive and discouraging exploration.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
